message:
chore(beads): close bd-3oj50 cross-agent Gemini session conversion bug

Record closure of bd-3oj50 in issue tracker. The bug involved cross-agent
X->Y session conversion failing for Gemini resume because the conversion
wrote to a hash-key project dir instead of the native project-key dir
used by Gemini list/resume, compounded by a set -u quoting bug in the
conversion command builder. Fix was verified with 35/35 E2E matrix checks
passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3lzu","title":"JFP: Add flywheel tool entry + metrics","description":"Add JeffreysPrompts (jfp) to the Flywheel page tool data.\n\nScope:\n- Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts: add a FlywheelTool entry for jfp with description, connections (ms, apr, cm), CLI commands, installCommand, and metadata.\n- Adjust flywheelDescription.subtitle/toolCount to match the new tool count.\n\nValidation:\n- bun run build (apps/web) if convenient; otherwise note not run.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:45:20.274651468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:50:01.878875498Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:50:01.878824732Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3moq","title":"Subtask: Add remediate() functions to doctor checks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:52.953602121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:44:38.331339230Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T23:44:38.331179739Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-31ps.6.2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3nbx","title":"acfs doctor --fix: Auto-remediation mode","description":"## Overview
Add `--fix` flag to `acfs doctor` that automatically remediates detected issues instead of just reporting them.

## Current Behavior
- `acfs doctor` runs health checks and reports problems
- User must manually fix each issue based on suggestions
- High friction for beginners who don't know HOW to fix things

## Proposed Behavior
- `acfs doctor --fix` attempts automatic remediation
- Safe fixes only: reinstall tools, fix permissions, regenerate configs
- Never destructive: won't delete user data or change system settings
- Falls back to manual instructions for unsafe operations

## Implementation Details
1. Modify `scripts/lib/doctor.sh` check functions to return remediation commands
2. Add `--fix` flag parsing to `scripts/commands/doctor.sh`
3. Each check needs: detect(), describe(), remediate()
4. Remediation functions wrapped in confirmation prompts
5. Dry-run mode: `--fix --dry-run` shows what would be done

## Safety Constraints
- NEVER delete user files
- NEVER modify ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc without backup
- Always confirm before system-level changes
- Log all remediation actions to ~/.local/share/acfs/doctor.log

## Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests for each remediation function
- [ ] E2E test: break tool, run doctor --fix, verify fixed
- [ ] Test --dry-run shows correct actions
- [ ] Test unsafe operations fall back to manual

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh (add remediate functions)
- scripts/commands/doctor.sh (add --fix flag)
- New: scripts/lib/remediation.sh (shared remediation helpers)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:11.394425192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:50.786428028Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:50.786408852Z","close_reason":"acfs doctor --fix already fully implemented: doctor_fix.sh with 6 fixers (path ordering, config copy, DCG hook, symlink create, plugin clone, ACFS sourcing), dry-run mode, undo support via autofix.sh, 25 passing unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3oj50","title":"Cross-agent X->Y session conversion fails for Gemini resume","description":"Real E2E matrix showed codex->gemini and claude->gemini conversions produced valid files but gemini --resume rejected IDs. Root cause: conversion wrote to hash-key project dir instead of native project-key dir used by Gemini list/resume; plus set -u quoting bug in conversion command builder blocked matrix execution.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:06:26.552548804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T02:06:27.541791348Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T02:06:27.541761883Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed conversion command quoting and Gemini native project-dir selection; strict real E2E matrix now passes (35/35 conversion-related checks, 0 failures).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3opa","title":"Deep exploration: Brenner Bot","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of Brenner Bot and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify Brenner installation
[[ -d /dp/brenner_bot ]] && echo "PASS: brenner repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: brenner repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v brenner &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: brenner command available" || { echo "FAIL: brenner not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check corpus location
[[ -d ~/.local/share/brenner/corpus ]] && echo "INFO: Corpus exists" || echo "INFO: No corpus yet"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/brenner-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/brenner_bot/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for research methodology docs, corpus format

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Research session management
  - Hypothesis tracking system
  - Corpus organization and search
  - Multi-agent research swarms
  - Integration with Claude Code

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
brenner --help 2>&1 | head -20
brenner search --help 2>&1 | head -10
brenner hypothesis --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
brenner list 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'brenner bot OR research session OR hypothesis'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'brenner research' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/brenner_bot/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/brenner_bot && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Research session structure
- [ ] Hypothesis tracking format
- [ ] Corpus organization
- [ ] Search capabilities
- [ ] Multi-agent coordination

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] cass - session mining
- [ ] xf - external research
- [ ] mail - research coordination
- [ ] ntm - multi-agent research

### 2.3 Corpus Verification
```bash
# Check corpus structure
ls -la ~/.local/share/brenner/corpus/ 2>/dev/null | head -10 || echo "No corpus yet"

# Check hypothesis tracking
brenner hypotheses 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No hypotheses command"
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update brenner entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Research session management
- `description`: Hypothesis-driven research
- `deepDescription`: How research workflow works
- `features`: Verified capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test brenner entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const brenner = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'brenner');
console.log('Testing brenner entry...');
console.assert(brenner, 'brenner entry exists');
console.assert(brenner.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(brenner.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Research Workflow
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/brenner-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== Brenner E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: List research sessions
echo "Test 1: List sessions..." | tee -a $LOG
brenner list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Search corpus
echo "Test 2: Search..." | tee -a $LOG
brenner search "test" --limit 3 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No search results"

# Test 3: Hypotheses
echo "Test 3: Hypotheses..." | tee -a $LOG
brenner hypothesis list 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No hypothesis command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-3opa --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Research workflow documented correctly
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:02:10.138573259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:39:55.530222451Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:39:55.530196993Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3te83","title":"Add ARM64 Linux binary to meta_skill releases","description":"meta_skill v0.1.0 release is missing aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu binary. When acfs doctor runs on ARM64 Linux (AWS Graviton, Raspberry Pi), the install script tries to download ms-0.1.0-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz which 404s.

Current release binaries:
- aarch64-apple-darwin (ARM64 macOS) present
- x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu (x86_64 Linux) present
- x86_64-pc-windows-msvc (Windows) present
- aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu (ARM64 Linux) MISSING

ROOT CAUSE: In /data/projects/meta_skill/.github/workflows/release.yml (lines 91-95), the ARM64 Linux target is commented out with note: "ARM Linux cross-compile requires vendored OpenSSL - see Cargo.toml"

FIX (in meta_skill repo at /data/projects/meta_skill):
1. Uncomment the aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu target in .github/workflows/release.yml:
   - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
     os: ubuntu-latest
     archive: tar.gz
     cross: true
2. Add cross-compilation support:
   - Install cross tool: cargo install cross --locked
   - Build with: cross build --release --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu --locked
   - OR: add openssl = { version = "0.10", features = ["vendored"] } to Cargo.toml
3. Cut new meta_skill release (v0.1.2+) that includes ARM64 Linux binary

FIX (in ACFS repo):
4. Update acfs doctor to show clear message when ARM64 Linux binary unavailable:
   "meta_skill ARM64 Linux binary not yet available. Install manually from https://github.com/.../releases or build from source: cargo install meta_skill"
5. Update acfs.manifest.yaml meta_skill entry to note ARM64 Linux support status

TESTS:

Build Tests (in meta_skill CI):
- test_arm64_linux_build: cross build --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu succeeds
- test_arm64_binary_runs: run binary in ARM64 Docker container (docker run --platform linux/arm64)
- test_release_includes_arm64: after release, verify aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz asset exists

Integration Tests (in ACFS):
- test_doctor_arm64_graceful: on ARM64 Linux, acfs doctor shows helpful message (not crash)
- test_install_arm64: full install script on ARM64 container successfully installs meta_skill

E2E Tests:
- test_arm64_graviton: run full ACFS install on AWS Graviton instance (or GitHub ARM runner)

GitHub issue: #115","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:08:40.264250256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:16:33.618142200Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:16:33.618121230Z","close_reason":"Fixed: ARM64 Linux target already uncommented (ac32c27), added AR env var for cross-compilation (ec2912f). Both commits pushed to meta_skill. Next release will include aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu binary.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":55,"issue_id":"bd-3te83","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ADDITIONAL TEST LOGGING REQUIREMENTS:
Build tests should log:
  - Target triple being built: 'Building for aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu'
  - Cross-compilation tool: 'Using cross v0.2.x' or 'Using cargo with vendored OpenSSL'
  - Binary size and architecture: 'Binary: 4.2MB, ELF 64-bit LSB, ARM aarch64'
  - file(1) output on the produced binary to verify architecture
  - Docker container platform: 'Testing in linux/arm64 container'
  - Binary execution result: './ms --version -> meta_skill 0.1.2'

Integration tests should log:
  - Detected architecture: 'uname -m -> aarch64'
  - Download URL attempted: 'https://github.com/.../ms-0.1.2-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz'
  - HTTP response code: '200 OK' or '404 Not Found'
  - acfs doctor output for meta_skill check

Final summary: 'ARM64 BUILD TESTS: N/N passed'
","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:11:15Z"}]}
